title: Aptamer compositions and the use thereof
abstract: Provided are aptamer compositions and their use for treating cancer.
claims: Claims WHAT IS CLAIMED IS: 1. A composition comprising an aptamer and an anticancer agent, wherein said aptamer is bound to said anticancer agent. 2. The composition of claim 1, wherein said anticancer agent is a small molecule, a nucleotide analogue, a nucleic acid, or a peptide. 3. The composition of claim 2, wherein said anticancer agent is a small molecule. 4. The composition of claim 3, wherein said small molecule is PBD, MMAE, DM1, oxaliplatin, irinotecan or doxorubicin. 5. The composition of claim 3, wherein said small molecule is bound to said aptamer via a linker. 6. The composition of claim 5, wherein said linker comprises a first nucleic acid hybridized to a second nucleic acid, wherein said first nucleic acid is covalently bound to said anticancer agent and said second nucleic acid is covalently bound to said aptamer. 7. The composition of claim 3, wherein said small molecule is covalently bound to said aptamer. 8 . The composition of claim 3, wherein said small molecule is non- covalently bound to said aptamer. 9. The composition of claim 2, wherein said anticancer agent is a nucleotide analogue, a nucleoside analogue, a pyrimidine analogue or a purine analogue. 10. The composition of claim 9, wherein said anticancer agent is gemcitabine, 5-flurouracil or a metabolite thereof. 11. The composition of claim 9, wherein said anticancer agent is gemcitabine monophosphate (dFdCMP) or 5-F-uridine monophosphate (5FdUMP). 12. The composition of claim 9, wherein said anticancer agent is present within the nucleic acid sequence of said aptamer. 13. The composition of claim 12, wherein said anticancer agent is gemcitabine or gemcitabine analogue, wherein said gemcitabine or gemcitabine analogue is present within the nucleic acid sequence at one or more cytidine positions. 14. The composition of claim 12, wherein said anticancer agent is 5- fluorouracil or 5-fluorouracil analogue, wherein said 5-fluorouracil or 5-fluorouracil analogue is present within the nucleic acid sequence at one or more uridine positions. 15. The composition of claim 1, wherein said composition is bound to a cellular receptor. 16. The composition of claim 15, wherein said cellular receptor is cell surface HSP70. 17. The composition of claim 16, wherein said HSP70 is mHSP70 (mortalin). 18. The composition of claim 15, wherein said cellular receptor is cell surface HSP90. 19. The composition of claiml5, wherein said cellular receptor is cell surface vimentin. 20. The composition of claim 15, wherein said cellular receptor is present on a cancer cell. 21. The composition of claim 20, wherein said cancer cell is a cancer stem cell. 22. The composition of claim 20, wherein said cancer cell is an undifferentiated cancer cell. 23. The composition of claim 22, wherein said cancer cell is an undifferentiated metastatic cancer cell. 24. The composition of claim 23, wherein said cancer cell is a pancreatic cancer cell, a glioblastoma cell, a colon cancer cell. 25. The composition of any one of claims 1 to 24, wherein said aptamer comprises a nucleic acid sequence of one of SEQ ID Nos: 1 to 7. 26. The composition of any one of claims 1 to 24, wherein said aptamer comprises a nucleic acid sequence having at least 80% sequence identity to one of SEQ ID Nos: 1 to 7. 27. The composition of any one of claims 1 to 24, wherein said aptamer comprises a nucleic acid sequence of SEQ ID NO: 8. 28. The composition of claim 1, wherein said aptamer comprises a nucleic acid sequence of SEQ ID NO:3, and said aptamer is bound to PBD, MMAE, DM1, oxaliplatin, irinotecan or doxorubicin. 29. The composition of claim 1, wherein said aptamer comprises a nucleic acid sequence of SEQ ID NO: 1, wherein one or more nucleotides of said nucleic acid sequence are replaced with dFdCMP or 5FdUMP. 30. The composition of claim 1, wherein said aptamer comprises a nucleic acid sequence of SEQ ID NO:2, wherein one or more nucleotides of said nucleic acid sequence are replaced with dFdCMP or 5FdUMP. 31. The composition of claim 1, wherein said aptamer comprises a nucleic acid sequence of SEQ ID NO:3, wherein one or more nucleotides of said nucleic acid sequence are replaced with dFdCMP or 5FdUMP. 32. The composition of claim 1, wherein said aptamer comprises a nucleic acid sequence of SEQ ID NO:4, wherein one or more nucleotides of said nucleic acid sequence are replaced with dFdCMP or 5FdUMP. 33. The composition of claim 1, wherein said aptamer comprises a nucleic acid sequence of SEQ ID NO:5, wherein one or more nucleotides of said nucleic acid sequence are replaced with dFdCMP or 5FdUMP. 34. The composition of claim 1, wherein said aptamer comprises a nucleic acid sequence of SEQ ID NO:6, wherein one or more nucleotides of said nucleic acid sequence are replaced with dFdCMP or 5FdUMP. 35. The composition of claim 1, wherein said aptamer comprises a nucleic acid sequence of SEQ ID NO:7, wherein one or more nucleotides of said nucleic acid sequence are replaced with dFdCMP or 5FdUMP. 36. A pharmaceutical formulation comprising the composition of any one of claims 1 to 35 and a pharmaceutically acceptable excipient. 37. A method of treating cancer, the method comprising administering to a subject in need thereof an effective amount of the composition of any one of claims 1 to 35. 38. The method of claim 37, wherein said cancer overexpresses HSP70 or HSP90 or vimentin. 39. The method of claim 37, wherein said cancer is an undifferentiated metastatic cancer. 40. The method of claim 37, wherein said cancer is pancreatic cancer, colon cancer or glioblastoma.
